title: CERAD Constructional Praxis Scoring
text: cat.cog automates CERAD Constructional Praxis scoring using vision-capable LLMs. CERAD widely-used cognitive assessment dementia research; one subtests asks subjects copy four geometric shapes (circle, diamond, two overlapping rectangles, cube). Standard scoring assigns 0–11 points based visible drawing features following published rubrics. cerad_drawn_score() sends image vision model, classifies relevant drawing features (closure, symmetry, intersection, depth cues, etc.), applies CERAD scoring rules return numeric score per drawing. function thin wrapper cat.stack::classify() image input, plus CERAD-specific scoring logic baked .

title: CERAD Constructional Praxis Scoring
text: returned data.frame one row per image integer score, raw classification scoring feature, image filename joining back participant records.
code: scores <- cerad_drawn_score(   shape       = "circle",   image_input = "./circle_drawings/",       # directory of PNG/JPG files   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model  = "gpt-4o"                    # vision-capable )  head(scores[, c("image_file", "score")])

what: Score a vector of individual files
title: CERAD Constructional Praxis Scoring
code: drawing_paths <- c(   "./participant_001_circle.png",   "./participant_002_circle.png",   "./participant_003_circle.png" )  scores <- cerad_drawn_score(   shape       = "circle",   image_input = drawing_paths,   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model  = "gpt-4o" )

title: CERAD Constructional Praxis Scoring
code: shapes <- c("circle", "diamond", "rectangles", "cube") max_scores <- c(circle = 2, diamond = 3, rectangles = 2, cube = 4) # Total possible: 11  for (s in shapes) {   scores <- cerad_drawn_score(     shape       = s,     image_input = file.path("./drawings", s),     api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),     user_model  = "gpt-4o"   )   saveRDS(scores, paste0("./scores_", s, ".rds")) }

title: CERAD Constructional Praxis Scoring
text: Scan photograph cleanly. Crop tightly drawing, correct rotation, ensure good contrast (dark drawing light background). Background noise (rulers, stamps, original reference shape page) can mislead model. Consistent format. PNG white background works best. Convert JPEGs cameras possible. One shape per image. include reference shape next subject’s drawing — model may distinguish . Resolution. ~500–1000 px long edge plenty; larger files just cost tokens without improving accuracy.

title: CERAD Constructional Praxis Scoring
text: Smaller models often miss subtle scoring features (e.g., whether rectangles actually intersect, whether cube visible depth lines). research use, gpt-4o claude-3-5-sonnet recommended mini/haiku tier.

title: CERAD Constructional Praxis Scoring
text: Always benchmark expert human scoring subsample trusting LLM scores scale. Compute weighted Cohen’s κ LLM clinician — values around 0.7+ generally acceptable secondary analysis; pre-registered primary outcomes warrant higher agreement.

title: CERAD Constructional Praxis Scoring
text: Full Getting Started guide: vignette("getting-started", package = "cat.llm") Function reference: ?cat.cog::cerad_drawn_score CERAD background: https://cerad.mc.duke.edu (Duke University CERAD) Validation methodology project Python README.

what: cat.cog — Cognitive assessment scoring with LLMs for R
title: cat.cog — Cognitive assessment scoring with LLMs for R
text: Cognitive assessment scoring LLMs. domain wrapper around cat.stack scores CERAD constructional praxis (drawn shape) assessments using vision-capable large language models. cat.cog wraps Python cat-cog package via reticulate.

title: cat.cog — Cognitive assessment scoring with LLMs for R
code: # From R-universe (recommended) install.packages("cat.cog",                  repos = c("https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev",                           "https://cloud.r-project.org"))  # Or from a local clone devtools::install("path/to/cat.stack") devtools::install("path/to/cat.cog")  # Install the Python backend (one-time setup) # pip install cat-cog

title: cat.cog — Cognitive assessment scoring with LLMs for R
code: library(cat.cog)  result <- cerad_drawn_score(   shape       = "circle",   image_input = "path/to/circle_drawing.png",   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: cat.cog — Cognitive assessment scoring with LLMs for R
code: results <- cerad_drawn_score(   shape       = "cube",   image_input = "path/to/cube_drawings/",   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model  = "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929",   model_source = "anthropic" )

what: Score CERAD constructional praxis drawings using LLMs — cerad_drawn_score
title: Score CERAD constructional praxis drawings using LLMs — cerad_drawn_score
text: Wraps Python cat_cog.cerad_drawn_score() function. Scores drawn shapes (circle, diamond, rectangles, cube) CERAD constructional praxis assessment using vision-capable LLMs.

title: Score CERAD constructional praxis drawings using LLMs — cerad_drawn_score
code: cerad_drawn_score(   shape,   image_input,   api_key,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   creativity = NULL,   safety = FALSE,   chain_of_thought = TRUE,   filename = NULL,   save_directory = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   ... )

title: Score CERAD constructional praxis drawings using LLMs — cerad_drawn_score
text: shape Character. shape scored: "circle", "diamond", "rectangles", "cube". image_input Character. Path image file directory images. api_key Character. API key model provider. user_model Character. Model name. Default "gpt-4o". creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature setting. Default NULL. safety Logical. Save progress item. Default FALSE. chain_of_thought Logical. Enable chain--thought reasoning. Default TRUE. filename Character NULL. Output CSV filename. Default NULL. save_directory Character NULL. Directory save results. Default NULL. model_source Character. Provider hint: "auto", "openai", "anthropic", "google", etc. Default "auto". ... Additional arguments passed Python function.

title: cat.cog 0.1.0
text: Initial release R interface Python catcog package. Specialized tools cognitive assessment scoring, including CERAD Constructional Praxis test evaluation dementia research. Exports cerad_drawn_score() scoring hand-drawn shapes via LLM vision models.
